title: rubus × erythraeus
intro: Rubus × erythraeus, also known as the Japanese raspberry or Himalayan raspberry, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a cross between the Japanese raspberry (Rubus ellipticus) and the Himalayan raspberry (Rubus ursinus). The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of phenolic compounds, including anthocyanins and ellagic acid. The health benefits of Rubus × erythraeus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Rubus × erythraeus can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and its leaves, stems, and fruits can be used to make jams, preserves, and other culinary products.
